name: Cappadocia
description: In this historic region, homes and churches are carved into ancient volcanic rock formations.

name: Basilica Cisterns of Istanbul
description: A marvel of Byzantine engineering under the streets of Istanbul.

name: Pamukkale Water Terraces
description: Roman ruins top white travertine terraces formed by ancient hot springs.

name: Lycian Rock Tombs
description: These tombs were built into the side of a mountain so that ancient angels could reach the dead.

name: Ottoman Bird Palaces
description: The ornate miniature mansions were built for Istanbul's feathered residents.

name: Myra Necropolis
description: A Lycian city of the dead carved into the hillside of Southern Turkey, complete with startlingly human touches.

name: Tombili
description: A meow-velous tribute to a beloved street cat, made famous on the internet.

name: Viking Runes at Hagia Sophia
description: A small etching on the white marble parapet was written in runic script by a Viking mercenary.

name: Gobekli Tepe
description: This hunter-gatherer architecture is believed to be the oldest religious complex known.

name: Tombs of the Sultans
description: Five sultans and their families rest in eternal splendor, teeming with overlooked beauty and drama.

name: Troy
description: This famous city eluded concrete detection until the early 2000's.

name: Kekova Island Sunken Ruins
description: Hidden just under the waters around this Turkish island are the ruins of a once great city.

name: Obelisk of Theodosius
description: This remarkably well-preserved Egyptian plinth is pretty well traveled for a giant piece of stone.

name: Camondo Stairs
description: A gorgeous, Gaudí-esque stairway constructed in the city of Istanbul by a prominent Jewish family.

name: Hagia Sophia Wishing Column
description: Legend says if you stick your thumb in the hole and it gets wet, your wish will be granted.

name: Pluto's Gate
description: A deadly ancient portal to the underworld.

name: Minyatür’s Nautical Instruments
description: Turkish adventurer antiques in the heart of the Grand Bazaar.

name: Nakilbent Cistern
description: A sixth-century Byzantine cistern hidden below a modern carpet store.

name: Ani Ghost City
description: An abused and forgotten metropolis, abandoned for centuries.

name: Piri Reis Map at Topkapi Palace
description: 1513 Turkish world map, full of European state secrets and snarky commentary.

name: Vlora Hanı
description: An Art Nouveau gem hides beneath the layers of city grime obscuring this once-magnificent building.

name: Abandoned Village of Kayakoy
description: A Turkish ghost town abandoned in a population exchange with Greece.

name: Derinkuyu Underground City
description: The deepest underground city in Turkey was designed to protect 20,000 people behind massive stone doors.

name: Termessos
description: Once described as the "Eagle's Nest," this ruined ancient city rests on a remote mountaintop.

name: Pudding Shop (Lale Restaurant)
description: This crucial gathering spot along the "hippie trail" still displays old notes between traveling beatniks and backpackers.

name: Yanartaş
description: Flames have been burning within this mountain for more than 2,500 years.

name: Cave of Zeus
description: This Turkish cave boasts some rather bizarre associations with the King of Mount Olympus.

name: City of Manazan
description: Naturally protected city carved from a rock face.

name: St. Stephen Bulgarian Iron Church
description: The most metal church of all time was built in record time because of an international, pious pissing contest.

name: Panorama 1453 Museum
description: Step back in time and witness the fall of Constantinople.

name: Tünel
description: Climb a steep hill in 90 seconds on the second-oldest urban subway in the world.

name: Gamirasu Cave Hotel
description: Live a night as a Byzantine King, with a minibar and private jacuzzi.

name: The Museum of Innocence
description: This museum was invented in a novel before being made real by the author.

name: Aspendos Theater
description: The best-preserved ancient theater in the world.

name: Kaymaklı Underground City
description: While not Turkey's deepest underground city, this subterranean redoubt is the largest.

name: Rumeli Hisari
description: This half-forgotten fortress was constructed by the Ottoman conqueror of Constantinople.

name: Safranbolu, Turkey
description: Ottoman-era houses lovingly preserved.

name: Ishak Pasha Palace
description: Standing proud atop a Turkish bluff, this Ottoman palace has retained its grandeur despite being abandoned for centuries.

name: Miniaturk
description: Large miniature theme park with 100-plus models.

name: Pierre Loti Hill
description: Have tea in a cemetery with a dazzling view of Istanbul.

name: Kaiser Wilhelm Fountain
description: A fountain standing as a testament to a doomed alliance.

name: Column of the Goths
description: This column celebrates Rome's victory over the Goths, and may be the oldest Roman monument in Istanbul.

name: Olympos Coastal National Park (Çıralı)
description: Roman ruins, natural methane fires and a lovely beach.

name: Burj Al Babas
description: The only residents of this abandoned housing development full of identical castles are stray cats and dogs.

name: Armenian Cathedral of the Holy Cross
description: An ancient church on a tiny island covered in stone bible stories has controversially been turned into a museum.

name: The Spoonmaker's Diamond
description: No one is sure if the world's fourth-largest diamond had been found in a rubbish heap or was pawned by Napoleon's mom.

name: Istanbul 2461
description: Quite possibly the oldest love poem known to humankind.

name: Sumela Monastery
description: A Turkish monastery tucked into a cliff wall.

name: Kuşadası
description: A charming beach town in Turkey captures all the colors of the rainbow.

name: Vedat Tek House
description: The eclectic home of a leading Turkish architect is a gorgeous piece of eye candy.

name: Serpent Column
description: Ancient serpentine sacrifice holder that has stood the test of time.

name: Çatalhöyük
description: The largest Neolithic ruins in the world hide a notably clean prehistoric metropolis.

name: Prinkipo Orphanage
description: One of the largest wooden constructions in Europe was meant to be a casino, but soon became an orphanage.

name: Nemrut Dagi
description: Mausoleum and Holy Seat of Antiochus I of Commagene.

name: Istanbul Railway Museum at Sirkeci Terminal
description: The legendary luxury train passage is commemorated with a charming museum inside its former eastern terminus.

name: Beylerbeyi Palace
description: This former Imperial summer home was the venue for a royal slap that once echoed 'round the world.

name: Harran Beehive Houses
description: The ancient abodes are a testament to human ingenuity amid harsh climates.

name: Museum of Energy
description: This shuttered power plant is now Turkey's first industrial archaeology museum.

name: Theodosian Wall Stone Ladder
description: Climb a set of vertiginous stone steps to the top of this impressive ancient edifice.

name: Pinara
description: At this ancient settlement at the foot of Mount Babadağ, honeycomb tombs are carved into a round cliff.

name: Pera Palace Hotel
description: This historic hotel preserves many treasures from its heyday hosting passengers on the Orient Express.

name: House of the Virgin Mary
description: The alleged house of the Virgin Mary is tucked away in a park and nature preserve on Mt. Koressos in Turkey and is holy to both Catholics and Muslims alike.

name: Büyükada
description: Over the centuries, this island paradise has done it all, from providing monks with ecclesiastical respite to sheltering an exiled Leon Trotsky.

name: Yedi Uyurlar Mağarası (Cave of the Seven Sleepers)
description: Seven early Christians were sealed into a cave where legend has it they slept unharmed for centuries.

name: Lycian Tomb Uçagiz
description: A mysterious ancient cemetery with Roman sarcophagi and Lycian inscriptions.

name: Yeralti Camii (Underground Mosque)
description: A surprising and unconventional mosque hidden in the subsoil of Istanbul.

name: Golden Horn Chain
description: A section of the long chain that blocked enemy ships from entering Constantinople.

name: Suluklugol
description: Fantastic lake on the heart of a fault line.

name: Balıklıgöl
description: A sacred lake with thousands of sacred carp and a biblical history.

name: Abandoned Turkish Bath
description: This hidden gem is bathed in beautiful natural light.

name: The Public Toilets of Ephesus
description: The ancient latrines of the Greeks, in all their public glory.

name: Brothel Advertisement at Ephesus
description: Carvings in Turkey's Marble Road may have directed ancient Romans to a hidden brothel.

name: Kanaat Lokantasi
description: This family-owned canteen has been selling goat milk ice cream since 1933.

name: Hasankeyf
description: This ancient city—home to nine civilizations—is soon to be buried under 200 feet of water.

name: Özkonak Underground City
description: This ancient subterranean megalopolis is known for the hot oil traps that were installed above each of its entrances.

name: Bar at Biz Istanbul
description: Eat your way through history.

name: Fortress of Kızkalesi
description: Mediterranean castle on a tiny Turkish island.

name: Sardis
description: These ruins once flourished as the capital of the Lydian kingdom, often credited with the invention of metal coinage.

name: Tlos
description: One of the most exquisite Lycian hilltop citadels in Turkey.

name: Hadrian’s Gate
description: This arch granting triumphal entrance into Antalya was designed to honor the emperor's visit.

name: Siirt Şeref Büryan
description: Come for the whole sides of slow-roasted lamb, stay for the regional Kurdish specialties.

name: Manisa Relief
description: This mysterious cliffside carving of an ancient Hittite deity has baffled scholars for centuries.

name: Agora of Smyrna
description: An underground network of massive stone arches and their skylit vaults support ruins of portico structures above.

name: Taksim Mosque
description: One of the newest mosques in a city filled with thousands.

name: Basilica of St. John
description: This crumbling medieval basilica once attracted pilgrims by the thousands to collect a miraculous dust that formed above the saint's tomb.

name: Meryem Ana Rum Ortodoks Kilesesi
description: It's believed that this small church was connected to the Hagia Sophia via a secret tunnel.

name: Baris Manco House and Museum
description: The home-turned-museum of Barış Manço, the mustachioed Turkish Elvis for children.

name: Buuzecedi
description: Homesick Syrian refugees head to this restaurant for a taste of Damascus.

name: Maiden's Sand Path of Kizkumu Beach
description: According to local legends, the shallow sandbar that allows visitors to walk across Orhaniye Bay has a tragic backstory.

name: Isa Yusuf Alptekin Park
description: A tiny park in the middle of some big power politics.

name: Nazaretian House
description: A beautiful 19th-century Armenian mansion is preserved above an unassuming cafe.

name: Church of St. Nicholas
description: This church houses the tomb—but not the remains—of St. Nicholas, the likely progenitor to Santa Claus.

name: Palace of the Porphyrogenitus
description: Byzantine emperors ruled, lived, and died in this 13th-century palace-turned-museum.

name: Inciralti Meyhanesi
description: Wash down heaping platters of meze with raki at one of the city's best taverns.

name: Kirkpinar Oil Wrestling Festival
description: Brawny guys rolling around in oil is the oldest regular sporting event besides the Olympics.

name: Haliç Metro Bridge
description: Taking the subway here means doing so over a bridge located in the middle of an estuary.

name: Istanbul Airport Museum
description: This airport also offers travelers the chance to view thousands of years of Turkish history.

name: Çamlik Railway Museum
description: This beautiful train graveyard is one of the largest collections of steam locomotives in the Mediterranean and Europe.

name: Durupınar Site
description: Natural rock formation, or biblical artifact that saved Earth's wildlife two by two?

name: Cathedral of Mren
description: This ancient abandoned church in a remote part of Turkey is in danger of falling over.

name: Susuz Caravanserai
description: A little-known caravanserai hidden among farmhouses in the Turkish countryside.

name: Lacivert
description: Feast on the freshest of fish by the Bosphorus.

name: Mount Judi
description: A small mountain at the crossroads of Turkey, Iraq, and Syria that some believe was the landing point of Noah's Ark.

name: Minuchir Mosque of Ani
description: Remnants of successive wars and Muslim colonialism in the 11th century.

name: Irgandi Bazaar Bridge
description: This Ottoman bridge is a rare covered bridge with shops, and considers itself the only bazaar bridge in the world.

name: Tuzluca Salt Mines
description: A former salt mine is now a cool picnic spot for families and asthmatics.

name: Asansör
description: This elevator was built in 1907 to connect two neighborhoods separated by a steep, rocky cliff.

name: Four-Legged Minaret
description: Balanced on four stone pillars, this historic tower was the site of a tragic political assassination.

name: Expo 2016 Antalya
description: A recently-abandoned international exhibition center.

name: Column of Marcian
description: The only Roman honorific column in Istanbul.

name: Ancient City of Magnesia
description: These ancient ruins include a stadium dating back to 400 BC.

name: Base of the Column of Arcadius
description: A massive marble pedestal is all that remains of a column that towered over Constantinople for 1300 years.

name: Çanakçı Heykeli
description: This giant work of Cappadocian pottery includes a memorial for the artisan who created it.

name: Land of Sleeping Volcanoes
description: Stand on the rim of a dormant volcano and gaze across a vast ash plain frozen in time.

name: Ahlat Seljuk Cemetery
description: Hundreds of medieval Islamic gravestones decorate the mountainous landscape in the largest cemetery in Turkey.

name: Çanakkale Trojan Horse
description: The legendary wooden horse that stands in modern Troy is a prop from the 2004 Wolfgang Petersen film.

name: Roman Baths of Ankara
description: This large Roman bath complex offers a window into ancient life in the arid Central Anatolian steppe.

name: Şemsi Pasha Mosque
description: An Ottoman-era mosque looking over the Black Sea.

name: Kirkkasik Bedesten
description: This 16th-century covered market is still open everyday.

name: Taşkuyu Mağarası (Taskuyu Cave)
description: This 470-meter-long dripstone cave was discovered by chance in 2006.

name: Anitkabir (Mausoleum of Kemal Atatürk)
description: Resting place and mausoleum of the founder and first president of Turkey.

name: Midas Tümülüsü (Tumulus MM)
description: The oldest known standing wooden building in the world is a chamber inside a burial mound.

name: Afyonkarahisar Castle
description: This 3,000-year-old “opium black fortress” towers over the ancient town named after it.

name: Librarian With the Donkey Monument
description: A monument to the Turkish librarian who delivered books on the back of a donkey.

name: Temple of Augustus
description: The ruins of a Roman temple from 1st Century BC with the only preserved inscription of “The Deeds of the Divine Augustus.”

name: Gökyay Chess Museum
description: A collection of over 700 chess sets in a beautiful historic building.

name: Şakirin Mosque
description: A mosque with distinctive modern features.

name: St Paul’s Well
description: A well said to have belonged to Paul the Apostle and part of UNESCO Tentative list of World Heritage Sites in Turkey.

name: The Amasya Mummies
description: A rare group of seven-century-old mummies preserved in astonishing detail.

name: Asur Ticaret Tableti
description: A giant replica of an Assyrian cuneiform tablet stands in the middle of a busy intersection near the city center of Kayseri.

name: Aslanhane Mosque
description: This 13th-century UNESCO-listed "lion's house" mosque is named after a repurposed lion sculpture.

name: Saruhan Bey Pine Tree
description: A centuries-old pine tree is growing through the floor and roof of this building.

name: Bozdağ Film Platoları
description: Step into the world of one of Turkey's most popular television series for a virtual trip through Ottoman Empire history.

name: Güvenlik Anıtı (Security Monument)
description: A monument completed by a Third Reich sculptor in 1935 in Ankara's central park encourages Turks to "be proud, work hard, and have trust."

name: Museum Market
description: This ancient tomb was discovered during the construction of a Turkish supermarket and was preserved.

name: Monument of the Great Triumph
description: A unique and controversial artifact of the early Turkish Republic.

name: Seyigazi Türbesi
description: A hilltop fortress full of tombs, including one for legendary hero Seyyid Battal Gazi.

name: Lületaşı Müzesi (Meerschaum Museum)
description: A museum celebrating the mineral used to make ornate, carved tobacco pipes.

name: Ulucanlar Prison Museum
description: A prison-turned-museum sheds light on modern Turkish history.

name: Uğur Mumcu Park
description: This harrowing memorial remembers a murdered investigative journalist.

name: Ulus Victory Monument
description: One of the earliest Ataturk monuments commemorates the victory of the Turkish War of Independence.

name: Asitane Restaurant
description: This eatery re-creates dishes from the Ottoman Empire so you can dine like a sultan.

name: Galata House Restaurant
description: Housed in an old British jail, this spot serves up Georgian, Russian, and Ukrainian specialities with a side of Turkish history.

name: Monument to Humanity, Kars
description: Unfinished monument to peace between Turkey and Armenia, long divided by a dispute over genocide.
